cases.sql: SELECT 1 FROM z
cases.expected: SELECT 1 FROM c.db.z AS z
cases.title: single table

cases.sql: SELECT 1 FROM y.z
cases.expected: SELECT 1 FROM c.y.z AS z
cases.title: single table with db

cases.sql: SELECT 1 FROM x.y.z
cases.expected: SELECT 1 FROM x.y.z AS z
cases.title: single table with db, catalog

cases.sql: SELECT 1 FROM x.y.z AS z
cases.expected: SELECT 1 FROM x.y.z AS z
cases.title: single table with db, catalog, alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM information_schema.tables
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.`information_schema.tables` AS tables
cases.title: only information schema

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM y.information_schema.tables
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.y.`information_schema.tables` AS tables
cases.title: information schema with db

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM x.y.information_schema.tables
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM x.y.`information_schema.tables` AS tables
cases.title: information schema with db, catalog

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM x.y.information_schema.tables AS z
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM x.y.`information_schema.tables` AS z
cases.title: information schema with db, catalog, alias

cases.sql: SELECT 1 FROM y.z AS z, z.a
cases.expected: SELECT 1 FROM c.y.z AS z, z.a
cases.title: redshift unnest syntax, z.a should be a column, not a table

cases.sql: SELECT results FROM Coordinates, coordinates.position AS results
cases.expected: SELECT results FROM c.db.Coordinates AS coordinates CROSS JOIN UNNEST(coordinates.position) AS results
cases.title: bigquery implicit unnest syntax, coordinates.position should be a column, not a table

cases.sql: SELECT results FROM db.coordinates, Coordinates.position AS results
cases.expected: SELECT results FROM c.db.coordinates AS coordinates CROSS JOIN UNNEST(Coordinates.position) AS results
cases.title: bigquery implicit unnest syntax, table is already qualified

cases.sql: WITH Coordinates AS (SELECT [1, 2] AS position) SELECT results FROM Coordinates, `Coordinates.position` AS results
cases.expected: WITH Coordinates AS (SELECT [1, 2] AS position) SELECT results FROM Coordinates AS Coordinates CROSS JOIN `c.Coordinates.position` AS results
cases.title: bigquery schema name clashes with CTE name - this is a join, not an implicit unnest

cases.sql: WITH a AS (SELECT 1 FROM z) SELECT 1 FROM a
cases.expected: WITH a AS (SELECT 1 FROM c.db.z AS z) SELECT 1 FROM a AS a
cases.title: single cte

cases.sql: WITH a AS (SELECT 1 FROM z) SELECT 1 FROM a CROSS JOIN a
cases.expected: WITH a AS (SELECT 1 FROM c.db.z AS z) SELECT 1 FROM a AS a CROSS JOIN a AS a
cases.title: two ctes that are self-joined

cases.sql: SELECT (SELECT y.c FROM y AS y) FROM x
cases.expected: SELECT (SELECT y.c FROM c.db.y AS y) FROM c.db.x AS x
cases.title: query that yields a single column as projection

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM x PIVOT (SUM(a) FOR b IN ('a', 'b'))
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.x AS x PIVOT(SUM(a) FOR b IN ('a', 'b')) AS _0
cases.title: pivoted table

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM x PIVOT (SUM(a) FOR b IN ('a', 'b')) AS piv
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.x AS x PIVOT(SUM(a) FOR b IN ('a', 'b')) AS piv
cases.title: pivoted table, pivot has alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (tbl)
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (c.db.tbl AS tbl)
cases.title: wrapped table without alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (tbl AS tbl)
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (c.db.tbl AS tbl)
cases.title: wrapped table with alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM ((((tbl AS tbl))))
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM ((((c.db.tbl AS tbl))))
cases.title: wrapped table with alias using multiple (redundant) parentheses

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (t1 CROSS JOIN t2)
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (c.db.t1 AS t1 CROSS JOIN c.db.t2 AS t2)
cases.title: wrapped join of tables without alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (t1 CROSS JOIN t2) AS t
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM c.db.t1 AS t1 CROSS JOIN c.db.t2 AS t2) AS t
cases.title: wrapped join of tables with alias, expansion of join construct

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM ((a CROSS JOIN b) CROSS JOIN c)
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM ((c.db.a AS a CROSS JOIN c.db.b AS b) CROSS JOIN c.db.c AS c)
cases.title: chained wrapped joins without aliases (1)

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (a CROSS JOIN (b CROSS JOIN c))
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (c.db.a AS a CROSS JOIN (c.db.b AS b CROSS JOIN c.db.c AS c))
cases.title: chained wrapped joins without aliases (2)

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM ((a CROSS JOIN ((b CROSS JOIN c) CROSS JOIN d)))
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM ((c.db.a AS a CROSS JOIN ((c.db.b AS b CROSS JOIN c.db.c AS c) CROSS JOIN c.db.d AS d)))
cases.title: chained wrapped joins without aliases (3)

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM ((a CROSS JOIN ((b CROSS JOIN c) CROSS JOIN (d CROSS JOIN e))))
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM ((c.db.a AS a CROSS JOIN ((c.db.b AS b CROSS JOIN c.db.c AS c) CROSS JOIN (c.db.d AS d CROSS JOIN c.db.e AS e))))
cases.title: chained wrapped joins without aliases (4)

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM ((a AS foo CROSS JOIN b AS bar) CROSS JOIN c AS baz)
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM ((c.db.a AS foo CROSS JOIN c.db.b AS bar) CROSS JOIN c.db.c AS baz)
cases.title: chained wrapped joins with aliases

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (tbl1 CROSS JOIN (SELECT * FROM tbl2) AS t1)
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (c.db.tbl1 AS tbl1 CROSS JOIN (SELECT * FROM c.db.tbl2 AS tbl2) AS t1)
cases.title: wrapped join with subquery without alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (tbl1 CROSS JOIN (SELECT * FROM tbl2) AS t1) AS t2
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM c.db.tbl1 AS tbl1 CROSS JOIN (SELECT * FROM c.db.tbl2 AS tbl2) AS t1) AS t2
cases.title: wrapped join with subquery with alias, parentheses cant be omitted because of alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM a LEFT JOIN (b INNER JOIN c ON c.id = b.id) ON b.id = a.id
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.a AS a LEFT JOIN (c.db.b AS b INNER JOIN c.db.c AS c ON c.id = b.id) ON b.id = a.id
cases.title: join construct as the right operand of a left join

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM a LEFT JOIN b INNER JOIN c ON c.id = b.id ON b.id = a.id
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.a AS a LEFT JOIN c.db.b AS b INNER JOIN c.db.c AS c ON c.id = b.id ON b.id = a.id
cases.title: nested joins

cases.sql: SELECT t.a FROM (tbl AS tbl) AS t
cases.expected: SELECT t.a FROM (SELECT * FROM c.db.tbl AS tbl) AS t
cases.title: parentheses cant be omitted because alias shadows inner table names

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM ((((tbl AS tbl)))) AS _0
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM c.db.tbl AS tbl) AS _0
cases.title: wrapped aliased table with outer alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (tbl1 AS tbl1 JOIN tbl2 AS tbl2 ON id1 = id2 JOIN tbl3 AS tbl3 ON id1 = id3) AS _0
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM c.db.tbl1 AS tbl1 JOIN c.db.tbl2 AS tbl2 ON id1 = id2 JOIN c.db.tbl3 AS tbl3 ON id1 = id3) AS _0
cases.title: join construct with three tables

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM ((tbl1 AS tbl1 JOIN tbl2 AS tbl2 ON id1 = id2 JOIN tbl3 AS tbl3 ON id1 = id3)) AS _0
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM c.db.tbl1 AS tbl1 JOIN c.db.tbl2 AS tbl2 ON id1 = id2 JOIN c.db.tbl3 AS tbl3 ON id1 = id3) AS _0
cases.title: join construct with three tables and redundant set of parentheses

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (tbl1 AS tbl1 JOIN (tbl2 AS tbl2 JOIN tbl3 AS tbl3 ON id2 = id3) AS _0 ON id1 = id3) AS _1
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM c.db.tbl1 AS tbl1 JOIN (SELECT * FROM c.db.tbl2 AS tbl2 JOIN c.db.tbl3 AS tbl3 ON id2 = id3) AS _0 ON id1 = id3) AS _1
cases.title: join construct within join construct

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM ((SELECT * FROM t))
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM ((SELECT * FROM c.db.t AS t) AS _0)
cases.title: wrapped subquery without alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM ((SELECT * FROM t1) INNER JOIN t2 ON a = b)
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM ((SELECT * FROM c.db.t1 AS t1) AS _0 INNER JOIN c.db.t2 AS t2 ON a = b)
cases.title: wrapped subquery without alias joined with a table

cases.sql: SELECT x FROM t, LATERAL UNNEST(t.xs) AS x
cases.expected: SELECT x FROM c.db.t AS t, LATERAL UNNEST(t.xs) AS x
cases.title: lateral unnest with alias

cases.sql: SELECT x FROM t, LATERAL UNNEST(t.xs)
cases.expected: SELECT x FROM c.db.t AS t, LATERAL UNNEST(t.xs) AS _0
cases.title: lateral unnest without alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM t CROSS JOIN JSON_ARRAY_ELEMENTS(t.response) WITH ORDINALITY AS kv_json
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.t AS t CROSS JOIN JSON_ARRAY_ELEMENTS(t.response) WITH ORDINALITY AS kv_json
cases.title: table with ordinality

cases.sql: ALTER TABLE t ADD PRIMARY KEY (id) NOT ENFORCED
cases.expected: ALTER TABLE c.db.t ADD PRIMARY KEY (id) NOT ENFORCED
cases.title: alter table

cases.sql: CREATE TABLE t1 AS (WITH cte AS (SELECT x FROM t2) SELECT * FROM cte)
cases.expected: CREATE TABLE c.db.t1 AS (WITH cte AS (SELECT x FROM c.db.t2 AS t2) SELECT * FROM cte AS cte)
cases.title: create statement with cte

cases.sql: DELETE FROM t1 WHERE NOT c IN (SELECT c FROM t2)
cases.expected: DELETE FROM c.db.t1 WHERE NOT c IN (SELECT c FROM c.db.t2 AS t2)
cases.title: delete statement

cases.sql: WITH cte AS (SELECT b FROM y) INSERT INTO s SELECT * FROM cte
cases.expected: WITH cte AS (SELECT b FROM c.db.y AS y) INSERT INTO c.db.s SELECT * FROM cte AS cte
cases.title: insert statement with cte

cases.sql: (SELECT x FROM t)
cases.expected: (SELECT x FROM c.db.t AS t)
cases.title: qualify wrapped query

cases.sql: SELECT db1.a.id, db2.a.id FROM db1.a JOIN db2.a ON db1.a.id = db2.a.id
cases.expected: SELECT a.id, a_2.id FROM c.db1.a AS a JOIN c.db2.a AS a_2 ON a.id = a_2.id
cases.title: replace columns with db/catalog refs

cases.sql: SELECT cat.db1.a.id, db2.a.id FROM cat.db1.a JOIN db2.a ON cat.db1.a.id = db2.a.id
cases.expected: SELECT a.id, a_2.id FROM cat.db1.a AS a JOIN c.db2.a AS a_2 ON a.id = a_2.id

cases.sql: COPY INTO (SELECT * FROM x) TO 'data' WITH (FORMAT 'CSV')
cases.expected: COPY INTO (SELECT * FROM c.db.x AS x) TO 'data' WITH (FORMAT 'CSV')

cases.sql: SELECT 1 FROM x TABLESAMPLE SYSTEM (10 PERCENT) CROSS JOIN y TABLESAMPLE SYSTEM (10 PERCENT)
cases.expected: SELECT 1 FROM c.db.x AS x TABLESAMPLE SYSTEM (10 PERCENT) CROSS JOIN c.db.y AS y TABLESAMPLE SYSTEM (10 PERCENT)
cases.title: tablesample

cases.sql: WITH cte_tbl AS (SELECT 1 AS col2) UPDATE y SET col1 = (SELECT * FROM x) WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM cte_tbl)
cases.expected: WITH cte_tbl AS (SELECT 1 AS col2) UPDATE c.db.y SET col1 = (SELECT * FROM c.db.x AS x) WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM cte_tbl AS cte_tbl)

cases.sql: WITH cte AS (SELECT 1 AS c, 'name' AS name) UPDATE t SET name = cte.name FROM cte WHERE cte.c = 1
cases.expected: WITH cte AS (SELECT 1 AS c, 'name' AS name) UPDATE c.db.t SET name = cte.name FROM cte WHERE cte.c = 1
cases.title: avoid qualifying CTE with UPDATE

cases.sql: WITH cte AS (SELECT 1 AS c, 'name' AS name) DELETE t FROM t AS t INNER JOIN cte ON t.id = cte.c
cases.expected: WITH cte AS (SELECT 1 AS c, 'name' AS name) DELETE c.db.t FROM c.db.t AS t INNER JOIN cte ON t.id = cte.c
cases.title: avoid qualifying CTE with DELETE

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM t
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.t AS _0
cases.title: canonicalize single table alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM t1 JOIN t2 ON t1.id = t2.id
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.t1 AS _0 JOIN c.db.t2 AS _1 ON _0.id = _1.id
cases.title: canonicalize join table aliases

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM db1.users JOIN db2.users ON db1.users.id = db2.users.id
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db1.users AS _0 JOIN c.db2.users AS _1 ON _0.id = _1.id
cases.title: canonicalize join with different databases

cases.sql: WITH cte AS (SELECT * FROM t) SELECT * FROM cte
cases.expected: WITH cte AS (SELECT * FROM c.db.t AS _0) SELECT * FROM cte AS _1
cases.title: canonicalize CTE alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM t)
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM c.db.t AS _0) AS _1
cases.title: canonicalize subquery alias

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM t1, (SELECT * FROM t2) AS sub, t3
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.t1 AS _2, (SELECT * FROM c.db.t2 AS _0) AS _1, c.db.t3 AS _3
cases.title: canonicalize multiple tables with subquery

cases.sql: WITH cte AS (SELECT * FROM t) SELECT * FROM cte PIVOT(SUM(c) FOR v IN ('x', 'y'))
cases.expected: WITH cte AS (SELECT * FROM c.db.t AS _0) SELECT * FROM cte AS _1 PIVOT(SUM(c) FOR v IN ('x', 'y')) AS _2
cases.title: canonicalize CTE with PIVOT

cases.sql: SELECT * FROM x WHERE x.a = (SELECT SUM(y.c) AS c FROM y WHERE y.a = x.a LIMIT 10)
cases.expected: SELECT * FROM c.db.x AS _1 WHERE _1.a = (SELECT SUM(_0.c) AS c FROM c.db.y AS _0 WHERE _0.a = _1.a LIMIT 10)
cases.title: canonicalize sources that reference external columns

cases.sql: SELECT t.foo FROM t AS t, (SELECT t.bar FROM t AS t)
cases.expected: SELECT _2.foo FROM c.db.t AS _2, (SELECT _0.bar FROM c.db.t AS _0) AS _1
cases.title: canonicalize sources that have colliding aliases

cases.sql: SELECT generate_series FROM GENERATE_SERIES(1,2)
cases.expected: SELECT generate_series FROM GENERATE_SERIES(1, 2) AS _0(generate_series)
cases.title: Qualify GENERATE_SERIES with its default column generate_series

cases.sql: SELECT g FROM GENERATE_SERIES(1,2) AS g
cases.expected: SELECT g FROM GENERATE_SERIES(1, 2) AS _0(g)
cases.title: Qualify GENERATE_SERIES with alias by wrapping it

cases.sql: SELECT g FROM GENERATE_SERIES(1,2) AS t(g)
cases.expected: SELECT g FROM GENERATE_SERIES(1, 2) AS t(g)
cases.title: Qualify GENERATE_SERIES with alias on table and columns

cases.sql: SELECT g FROM GENERATE_SERIES(1,2) AS t(g)
cases.expected: SELECT g FROM GENERATE_SERIES(1, 2) AS _0(g)
cases.title: Qualify GENERATE_SERIES with explicit column and canonicalize_table_aliases

cases.sql: SELECT jsonvalue.id FROM deal, JSONB_TO_RECORDSET(CAST(deal.type AS JSONB)) AS jsonvalue(id INT, key VARCHAR, text VARCHAR)
cases.expected: SELECT _1.id FROM c.db.deal AS _0, JSONB_TO_RECORDSET(CAST(_0.type AS JSONB)) AS _1(id INT, key VARCHAR, text VARCHAR)
cases.title: Qualify JSONB_TO_RECORDSET with typed alias columns and canonicalize_table_aliases

cases.sql: CREATE TEMPORARY FUNCTION f() RETURNS STRING AS ((((SELECT 'foo'))))
cases.expected: CREATE TEMPORARY FUNCTION c.db.f() RETURNS STRING AS ((SELECT 'foo'))
cases.title: Preserve wrapping parens of a query used as a function body
